questions.question: Explain the difference between Computer Architecture and Computer Organization. Give one example of each.
keyPoints: Computer Architecture is the abstract design including instruction set, addressing modes, and data types
Computer Organization is the actual hardware implementation showing how components are connected and operate
Example of architecture: Instruction set design
Example of organization: Use of cache memory, bus structure

questions.question: Draw and explain the Fetch-Decode-Execute cycle for instruction execution.
keyPoints: Three main phases: Fetch, Decode, Execute
Fetch: Get instruction from memory, update PC
Decode: Interpret the instruction, generate control signals
Execute: Perform the operation using ALU or move data

questions.question: What are the main components of the Central Processing Unit (CPU)? Explain the function of each.
keyPoints: Control Unit: Generates control signals, directs operations
ALU: Performs arithmetic and logical operations
Registers: High-speed storage for data and addresses
These components work together to execute instructions

questions.question: Explain the concept of memory hierarchy. Why is it important for computer performance?
keyPoints: Memory hierarchy organizes storage by speed and capacity
Levels: Registers, Cache, RAM, Secondary storage
Faster memory is smaller and more expensive
Improves performance by keeping frequently used data in faster storage

questions.question: What are the three types of buses in a computer system? Explain the function of each.
keyPoints: Address Bus: Carries memory addresses from CPU to memory
Data Bus: Carries actual data between components
Control Bus: Carries control signals for coordination
Each bus is unidirectional or bidirectional as required

questions.question: Explain the purpose and function of the following CPU registers: Program Counter, Instruction Register, and Accumulator.
keyPoints: PC: Holds address of next instruction to be fetched
IR: Holds current instruction being executed
ACC: Stores intermediate results of arithmetic operations
These registers work together during instruction execution

questions.question: What are addressing modes? Explain immediate addressing and direct addressing with examples.
keyPoints: Addressing modes specify how to locate operand data
Immediate: Operand value is included in instruction (e.g., ADD #5)
Direct: Address of operand is given in instruction (e.g., LOAD 1000)
Different modes provide flexibility in data access

questions.question: With the help of a block diagram, explain the internal organization of a simple computer system. Show how the CPU, memory, and I/O components are connected.
keyPoints: Draw block diagram showing CPU, Memory, I/O connected via system bus
Explain CPU components: CU, ALU, Registers
Explain bus structure and data flow
Show fetch-execute cycle interactions between components
Discuss address and data path connections

questions.question: Explain the complete sequence of operations performed by the CPU during the execution of an instruction. Use a specific example like ADD M to illustrate.
keyPoints: Describe fetch phase: PC to MAR, memory read, IR gets instruction
Describe decode phase: Control unit interprets opcode
Describe execute phase: MAR gets operand address, memory read, ALU performs addition, result stored in accumulator
Explain how PC is updated for sequential execution

questions.question: Discuss the differences between RAM and ROM. Also explain the various types of ROM and their characteristics.
keyPoints: RAM: Volatile, read/write, used for temporary storage
ROM: Non-volatile, read-only, used for permanent instructions
Types of ROM: PROM (programmable once), EPROM (erasable), EEPROM (electrical erase)
Applications and characteristics of each type

questions.question: What is an instruction set? Discuss the different categories of instructions with examples.
keyPoints: Instruction set is the collection of all machine instructions
Categories: Data Transfer (MOV, LOAD, STORE), Data Processing (ADD, SUB, AND, OR), Control Flow (JUMP, BRANCH, CALL), I/O (IN, OUT)
Explain each category with examples
Discuss importance of instruction set design
